summary: Strengthened Layer 2 rerun on desktop, mobile-chrome, and mobile-ios-small caught four concrete UX/content issues on the shared HOME/RECIPE_DETAIL surface. The remaining gap is native sort option popup readability, which still needs either real-device inspection or a custom control.

findings.title: HOME heading hierarchy and copy feel overly long on small mobile
findings.expected: The first-screen headings should scan quickly on mobile and avoid sounding verbose or awkward.
findings.actual: On the small mobile HOME shell, the top banner headline and the discovery headline together create a long, crowded first impression before the search controls.
repro_steps: Observe the top banner headline and the HOME discovery heading before interacting with controls.

findings.title: Recipe detail stats collapse into a stacked layout that burns scroll on mobile
findings.expected: Core recipe stats should stay compact and easy to scan without forcing unnecessary vertical scrolling on mobile.
findings.actual: On mobile and especially mobile-ios-small, the stats area becomes a tall stacked block that pushes more important interaction content farther down the screen.
repro_steps: Open RECIPE_DETAIL on mobile-chrome or mobile-ios-small.
Inspect the stats block directly under the title and compare how much vertical space it consumes before the ingredient section.

findings.title: Recipe detail actions are separated from their related stats by the ingredients block
findings.expected: Actions such as planner, like, save, and share should sit close to the primary recipe information they act on.
findings.actual: The user sees the title and stats first, then the ingredient block, and only after that reaches the main action row, which breaks information hierarchy and adds extra scroll.
repro_steps: Open RECIPE_DETAIL on mobile-chrome or desktop.
Follow the page from title and stats to ingredients and then down to the action row.

findings.title: Recipe detail exposes duplicate share actions on the same screen
findings.expected: A single clear share affordance should exist for the same action on one screen.
findings.actual: The detail hero already contains a share button, but the lower action row repeats another share button, creating duplicate CTA clutter.
repro_steps: Open RECIPE_DETAIL on desktop or mobile-chrome.
Inspect the hero controls and the lower action row on the same screen.

remainingRisks: Sort option popup readability on mobile remains a Layer 2 blind spot because the native select sheet is rendered outside the page canvas. The closed control itself is readable, but the actual option popup still needs real-device inspection or a custom control.

checklistCoverage.notes: Covered in the 2026-04-01 exploratory rerun with current UI evidence and deterministic suite cross-checks.
